## Source Record

- Source: public GitHub repository homepage for `wyattowalsh/nbadb`
- Scope captured: repository positioning, public metadata, visible top-level layout, and release/distribution links

## Why It Matters

This is the highest-signal public release surface for nbadb. It exposes the project narrative, shows the active code and docs layout, and links directly to the downstream distribution surfaces that matter for adoption: Kaggle, docs, and package install instructions.

## Key Excerpts

> "The most comprehensive open NBA database available."

> "All data spans from the 1946-47 season to present (auto-updating via the daily pipeline)."

> Visible public repo metadata at capture time included `59` stars, `14` forks, `315` commits, and top-level paths such as `docs`, `notebooks`, `src/nbadb`, and `tests`.

> "Data Extraction (from https://stats.nba.com) and Processing Scripts to Produce the NBA Database on Kaggle (https://kaggle.com/wyattowalsh/basketball)"

## Capture Notes

- GitHub HTML included a large amount of page chrome, but the repository payload was still readable enough to retain repo-level facts.
- The homepage is especially valuable because it consolidates quick-start install/run guidance with links to the docs site and Kaggle dataset.
- Visible project layout confirms nbadb is distributed as code, docs, notebooks, and generated dataset artifacts rather than as a single-file package only.
